title: Recipe for Disaster/Freeing Sir Amik Varze

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: To protect this noble knight from the magic of the culinaromancer, you will have to give him a creme brulee.

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Not just any creme brulee mind you, but a creme brulee supreme!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well that doesn't sound too hard. What's so supreme about a creme brulee supreme?

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Are you going to constantly ask me questions?

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: I am a fortune-teller, not a cook, and again I would suggest you speak to the cook next door who will be much more helpful on such culinary matters.

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Although I happen to know that one of the ingredients is the egg of the evil chicken.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The evil chicken? Great. He only ever seems to show up when I don't want him to.

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: I believe the wise old man in Draynor has studied the Evil Chicken.

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: I would suggest you speak to him to get you on the right track. And tell him Bubbykins says hi.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... Bubbykins?

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: That is not important right now. Go! Protect Sir Amik from the magic that threatens his life, my spell will only hold so long!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Just give me 5 minutes and I'll be back!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you give me that recipe again, please?

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Certainly, forgetful hero.

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: To protect this noble knight from the magic of the culinaromancer, you will have to give him a creme brulee.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well that doesn't sound too hard. What's so supreme about a creme brulee supreme?

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: Are you going to constantly ask me questions?

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: I am a fortune-teller, not a cook, and again I would suggest you speak to the cook next door who will be much more helpful on such culinary matters.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The evil chicken? Great. He only ever seems to show up when I don't want him to.

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: I believe the wise old man in Draynor has studied the Evil Chicken.

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: I would suggest you speak to him to get you on the right track. And tell him Bubbykins says hi.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... Bubbykins?

lines.speaker: Aris
lines.text: That is not important right now. Go! Protect Sir Amik from the magic that threatens his life, my spell will only hold so long!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Just give me 5 minutes and I'll be back!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can we talk about what happened in the dining room?

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Wow! You were incredible! The way you defeated the Culinaromancer, you made it seem so easy!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So... I don't suppose you can remember exactly how I defeated him can you?

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Of course! Which bit can't you remember doing?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That white knight... Sir Amik Varze... I can't quite remember how I saved him...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: but it was something to do with creme brulee supreme...

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Ah yes! Why, I was so impressed with that recipe you created that I memorised it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah, you did, did you? I don't suppose you could refresh my memory about that?

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: But of course! It was an unusual dish, an unusual dish indeed! First you blend together, in a pot, some milk and some cream. Then add cornflour to the mixture.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Cornflour? How do I... er, how DID I make that then?

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Oh, you told me you used sweetcorn in a mill!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Really? Weird. Okay, what did I do then?

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Then you travelled to the Kharazi Jungle and searched the plants there until you found a vanilla stick, which was then added to the mixture.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: After that you told me that you sought out and killed the fearsome Evil Chicken, to get one of his eggs, which was then mixed with the other ingredients.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Finally you made some cinnamon by grinding the branch of a Dramen Tree and sprinkled it on top of the creme brulee.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds easy enough so far. Er, I mean it was easy enough up to that point.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Yes, but then came the impressive part!

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Apparently, a creme brulee supreme needs to be flambed at exactly the right temperature, and the only fire that is hot enough to do this is the breath of a dragon. Luckily, while killing black dragons near the Evil

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Chicken's lair, you came across a method of forcing a dragon to your will so that it would complete the dish, which you then used to protect Sir Amik!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I definitely said that I had to kill some black dragons to do that?

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Oh yes. You were quite adamant about it. Specifically black dragons. Specifically near the Evil Chicken's lair.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh yeah... I remember now...

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Did you want a reminder on any other of your brave exploits?

sections.title: Talking to the Wise Old Man

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Greetings, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you're a wise old man, huh?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Less of the 'old' man, if you please!

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: But yes, I suppose you could say that. I prefer to think of myself as a sage.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what's a sage doing here?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I've spent most of my life studying this world in which we live. I've strode through the depths of the deadliest dungeons, roamed the murky jungles of Karamja, meditated on the glories of Saradomin on Entrana,

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: and read dusty tomes in the Library of Varrock.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Now I'm not as young as I used to be, I'm settling here where it's peaceful.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: It's a pity about that vampyre that keeps attacking the village. At least Saradomin protects me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's quite an exciting life you've had.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Exciting? Yes, I suppose so.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Now I'm here, perhaps I could offer you the benefit of my experience and wisdom?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks! So how can you help me?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Well, I imagine you've gathered up quite a lot of stuff on your travels. Things you used for quests a long time ago that you don't need now.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: If you like, I can look through your bank and see if there's anything you can chuck away.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Alternatively, you can bring items here and show them to me. If I see that it's something you don't need, I'll let you know. I might even be willing to buy it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you'll help me clear junk out of my bank?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Yes, that's right. Or I'd be happy to chat with you about the wonders of this world!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd just like to ask you something.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Please do!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you tell me about the Evil Chicken, please?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: The Evil Chicken! Many are the myths and legends surrounding this foul bird. Some say that it is the result of a mad mage's attempt to summon a demon, others say that it was once a

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: normal chicken that realised how limited its farmyard life was. It threw off its chains and attempted to unite the other chickens in revenge against their human captors.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: When that failed the Evil Chicken grew angry with its brethren and swore to kill any human or chicken that it ever encountered again!

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: It is whispered that there is a coven of witches that meet, on the night of the full moon in the fabled fairy kingdom, to worship this bird and that they can summon it to do their twisted will!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, so if you were looking for the chicken where would you go?

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: I would start in the lands of the fairies, possibly there is some truth in the rumours of summoning the chicken there. Actually, I've done further research, it may be around

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: here somewhere, if you're really thinking of going there you should be extremely careful, it's claimed that the chicken has forced some very powerful creatures to guard his lair.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: Is there anything else you'd like to ask?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks, maybe some other time.

lines.speaker: Wise Old Man
lines.text: As you wish. Farewell, [player name].

sections.title: Using an egg on the Chicken Shrine in Zanaris
Searching Evil Chicken's nest after losing the egg
Rubbing Dragon token while not in Zanaris or Lumbridge
Rubbing Dragon token while in Zanaris or Lumbridge

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What happened? I rubbed that coin and everything went black!

lines.speaker: K'klik
lines.text: Who are you? You're not the hero I gave my token to!

lines.speaker: K'klik
lines.text: How did you get that coin you used to summon me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I took it from the dead body of a black dragon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I didn't realise what it would do if I rubbed it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who, or what, are you anyway?

lines.speaker: K'klik
lines.text: My name is K'klik. I am a Fairy Dragon.

lines.speaker: K'klik
lines.text: One of the last surviving members of my race, I fear.

lines.speaker: K'klik
lines.text: Enough of that: you summoned me, so how can I help?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid I don't understand.

lines.speaker: K'klik
lines.text: The person who holds my token can summon me by rubbing it.

lines.speaker: K'klik
lines.text: They then have the right to request one thing of me before returning my token.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So I can ask one thing of you?

lines.speaker: K'klik
lines.text: That's right. What do you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Please flambe this creme brulee for me.

lines.speaker: K'klik
lines.text: Sure, that's easy.

lines.speaker: K'klik
lines.text: I think you need to wear some sort of protective gloves, otherwise I'll flambé your arm.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Go ahead, I'll be fine.

lines.speaker: K'klik
lines.text: Okay, if you're sure.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What gloves do you have in mind?

lines.speaker: K'klik
lines.text: A pair of ice gloves should do the trick.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll go fetch them then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could I just keep your token for when I really need it, please?

lines.speaker: K'klik
lines.text: I suppose that seems fair. Alright, just call me when you need me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I wish for some xp?

lines.speaker: K'klik
lines.text: Sorry, can't help. You'll have to ask the genie for that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd like a horse, please.

lines.speaker: K'klik
lines.text: No horses in Gielinor. Try again.

sections.title: Using milk with a pot of cream
Using pestle and mortar on a dramen branch
Using the brulee supreme on Sir Amik Varze
